action.reasoning: Observation: Environment works well. Nodes have utility values (frontier cells observable), visited flags, and neighbor lists. The current greedy heuristic picks by utility/distance. Analysis: The score formula rewards success AND short paths. I need to maximize success rate first, then minimize distance. A better strategy: use utility-weighted greedy with backtracking to unvisited high-utility areas. Also, I should consider that the episode ends when no nodes have utility>0, so I need to ensure full coverage. Decision: Improve the heuristic planner with better lookahead - pick highest utility neighbor, but if all neighbors have 0 utility, backtrack to nearest unvisited high-utility node. Submit eval immediately to get baseline.
